title: Paranasal Sinuses
body_html:
Overview

The paranasal sinuses are paired structures and include the following:
  • Maxillary sinuses
  • Ethmoidal sinuses
  • Frontal sinuses
  • Sphenoidal sinuses

Key Points
  • They are lined with ciliated pseudostratified columnar epithelium (respiratory epithelium)
  • The maxillary sinuses are the largest of the paranasal sinus group
  • The paranasal sinuses are innervated by branches of the trigeminal nerve

Image sourced from Wikipedia

Mucus Drainage
  • The frontal and maxillary sinuses drain into the middle meatus
  • The sphenoid sinuses drain into the sphenoethmoidal recess
  • The ethmoidal sinuses drain into the middle and superior meatus


Development & Expected Knowledge
  • The college expect candidates to be able to identify the sinuses on plain XRAY and also have an understanding of their development from age 7 onwards
  • Enlargement is via excavation of bone by pneumatic diverticula

SinusFirst DetectedDevelopment
EthmoidPresent at birthgrow and pneumatize until the age of 12
MaxillaryPresent at birth2nd growth phase between 6-12 inferiorly towards teeth
SphenoidDetected at 3-4 yearsFrom age 7 extends posteriorly, completing growth in teenage years
FrontalDetected at 7-12 yearsGrowth completed by age 20
